articles.title: Historic decline in U.S. overdose deaths threatened by changing street drug supply
articles.summary: Drug overdose deaths are plummeting in the U.S. in ways never seen before. Experts worry new, toxic "synthetic" street drugs could derail the recovery.

articles.title: Daring and Dazzling, a New LACMA Floats Above Los Angeles
articles.summary: After $724 million and a decade of battles, the pugnacious David Geffen Galleries reassert the city’s role as a petri dish for experimental design.

articles.title: Veteran diplomat offers insights into the war in Iran — and thoughts on what's next
articles.summary: The war entered a new phase when President Trump began a U.S. naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Aaron David Miller of the Carnegie Endowment for International Peace explains what this means.

articles.title: House Republicans Step Up Scrutiny of ActBlue Over Foreign Donations
articles.summary: Three Republican-led committees, responding to a New York Times report this month, accused the Democratic fund-raising organization of withholding documents from a subpoena request.

articles.title: At a House Republican’s New York Event, Flashes of an Iran Backlash
articles.summary: Representative Mike Lawler, who is seeking re-election in a swing district in the Hudson Valley, faced tough questions from constituents about his stance on the war in Iran.

articles.title: Virginia Governor Ends Tax Breaks for Confederate Groups
articles.summary: The new law signed by Gov. Abigail Spanberger is the culmination of a long, Democrat-led push to distance Virginia from its Confederate past.

articles.title: You can order your own blood work now. Interpreting the results is another story
articles.summary: Firms like Function Health and Oura market regular blood tests to people wanting to take their health into their own hands. The process often raises more questions for patients than it can answer.

articles.title: After losing loved ones, an Israeli and a Palestinian work together for Middle East peace
articles.summary: An Israeli whose parents were killed on Oct. 7, 2023, and a Palestinian whose brother died from injuries in Israeli custody say they've become like brothers. Their new book is The Future Is Peace: A S

articles.title: Fuel protests have Ireland's government facing possible no-confidence vote
articles.summary: The prime minister announced new tax cuts to try to end the crisis that began after the U.S.-Israel war on Iran led to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. The government could face a no-confidence vo

articles.title: Middle East War Will Slow Global Economic Growth, I.M.F. Warns
articles.summary: The conflict could also fuel another bout of inflation, according to the International Monetary Fund.

articles.title: Millions of people are pretending to be AI chatbots — for fun
articles.summary: Websites like youraislopbores.me have become playgrounds for people looking for light relief in a bot-heavy world.

articles.title: Virginia joins a national effort to ensure only popular vote winners become president
articles.summary: With Virginia on board, the National Popular Vote Compact is now enacted in states worth 222 electoral votes. Here's what that means.

articles.title: Eric Swalwell will resign from Congress. And, Trump feuds with Pope Leo over Iran War
articles.summary: Eric Swalwell is resigning from Congress after multiple women accused him of sexual assault and misconduct. And, Trump is feuding with Pope Leo, calling him weak on crime for opposing the war.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon are holding rare diplomatic talks in D.C.
articles.summary: An official briefed on Israel's strategy for the talks described Tuesday's meeting as "preparatory" and aimed at laying out a framework for future negotiations.

articles.title: Olivia Troye, Ex-Pence Aide, Runs for the House as a Democrat
articles.summary: After spending most of her Washington career as a Republican, she joins a crowded field of Democrats running in a Virginia district that doesn’t exist yet.

articles.title: A Divided America Processes a War With Iran
articles.summary: As the war in Iran extends into its seventh week and a truce feels increasingly shaky, many Americans expressed bewilderment about a conflict that came with little warning.

articles.title: Ukraine hopes for more cooperation after Hungary elects new prime minister
articles.summary: The incoming prime minister of Hungary is no fan of Ukraine, but he says he wants to work with the European Union, raising hopes he'll lift a Hungarian veto on a $100 billion EU loan to Kyiv.

articles.title: 'Hanging by a thread': Fertilizer shortage in India deepens due to ongoing Iran war
articles.summary: With shipments through the Strait of Hormuz stalled after the Iran war began, fertilizer shortages are deepening across India.

articles.title: U.K. inquiry lays bare 'catastrophic' missed chances before stabbings at dance class
articles.summary: An inquiry found that a mass killing by a British teenager in 2024 at a Taylor Swift-themed dance class could have been prevented if his parents and state agencies had acted on his violence fixation.

articles.title: Eric Swalwell Says He Will Resign From Congress After Sex Abuse Accusations
articles.summary: The California lawmaker said in a social media post Monday that he would quit. He has denied accounts of abusing women.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
